sections.title: All Things Entrusted to the Son

verses.1: After Jesus had finished instructing His twelve disciples, He went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.
verses.2: Meanwhile John heard in prison about the works of Christ, and he sent his disciples
verses.3: to ask Him, “Are You the One who was to come, or should we look for someone else?”

segments.text: “Go back and report to John what you hear and see:
segments.speaker: Jesus

5.text: “The blind receive sight, the lame walk, those with leprosy are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the good news is proclaimed to the poor.
5.speaker: Jesus

6.text: “Blessed is the one who does not fall away on account of Me.”
6.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As John’s disciples were leaving, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John:
“What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed swaying in the wind?
segments.speaker: Jesus

8.text: “Otherwise, what did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? Look, those who wear fine clothing are found in kings’ palaces.
8.speaker: Jesus

9.text: “What then did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
9.speaker: Jesus

10.text: “This is the one about whom it is written: ‘Behold, I will send My messenger ahead of You, who will prepare Your way before You.’
10.speaker: Jesus

11.text: “Truly I tell you, among those born of women there has risen no one greater than John the Baptist. Yet even the least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
11.speaker: Jesus

12.text: “From the days of John the Baptist until now, the kingdom of heaven has been subject to violence, and violent people have been raiding it.
12.speaker: Jesus

13.text: “For all the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John.
13.speaker: Jesus

14.text: “And if you are willing to accept it, he is the Elijah who was to come.
14.speaker: Jesus

15.text: “He who has ears, let him hear.
15.speaker: Jesus

16.text: “To what can I compare this generation? They are like children sitting in the marketplaces and calling out to others:
16.speaker: Jesus

17.text: “‘We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we sang a dirge, and you did not mourn.’
17.speaker: Jesus

18.text: “For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, ‘He has a demon!’
18.speaker: Jesus

19.text: “The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, ‘Look at this glutton and drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’ But wisdom is vindicated by her actions.”
19.speaker: Jesus

verses.20: Then Jesus began to denounce the cities in which most of His miracles had been performed, because they did not repent.

21.text: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
21.speaker: Jesus

22.text: “But I tell you, it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon on the day of judgment than for you.
22.speaker: Jesus

23.text: “And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted up to heaven? No, you will be brought down to Hades! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Sodom, it would have remained to this day.
23.speaker: Jesus

24.text: “But I tell you that it will be more bearable for Sodom on the day of judgment than for you.”
24.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “I praise You, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because You have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children.
segments.speaker: Jesus

26.text: “Yes, Father, for this was well-pleasing in Your sight.
26.speaker: Jesus

27.text: “All things have been entrusted to Me by My Father. No one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal Him.
27.speaker: Jesus

28.text: “Come to Me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.
28.speaker: Jesus

29.text: “Take My yoke upon you and learn from Me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
29.speaker: Jesus

30.text: “For My yoke is easy and My burden is light.”
30.speaker: Jesus

metadata.source: Derived from the Berean Standard Bible (BSB). Missing verses restored from the Textus Receptus tradition for completeness.
